title: Fixture-id path traversal: 015 F-P1-9 guard only hardened run-benchmark.cjs, not the materializer that writes fixture outputs first
evidence: materialize-benchmark-fixtures.cjs:91 does fs.writeFileSync(path.join(outputsDir, `${fixture.id}.md`), ...) with no sanitization. loop-host.cjs:155-158 runs the materializer BEFORE run-benchmark.cjs, and the auto YAML step_run_benchmark mirrors this. Empirically: a fixture with id="../escaped" caused the materializer to write escaped.md OUTSIDE outputsDir (into its parent) and exit 0. The 015 regression test run-benchmark-hardening.vitest.ts:67-79 only calls runBenchmark without the materializer, so its `evil.md not created` assertion does not cover the real first-writer path; grep confirms zero materializer-traversal test coverage.
fix: Port assertSafeFixtureId / SAFE_FIXTURE_ID from run-benchmark.cjs into materialize-benchmark-fixtures.cjs and call it before path.join(outputsDir, `${fixture.id}.md`) at line 91 (and ideally share one helper). Add a materializer hostile-id regression test.
claim: The 014 F-P1-9 fixture-id traversal class is only partially remediated: the guard exists in run-benchmark.cjs but not in the materializer, which is the first and actual writer of fixture output files in the model-benchmark plan.
counterevidenceSought: Checked whether run-benchmark.cjs guard runs before any materializer write (it does not — separate process, runs second); checked for any sanitization or mkdir-guard inside the materializer (none); checked tests for materializer hostile-id coverage (none); ran empirical repro confirming the parent-dir escape write.
alternativeExplanation: Could be argued benign because benchmark profiles are trusted-author content, so a hostile fixture id requires a malicious/compromised profile author. Rejected as a downgrade-to-P2 because the project itself treats the analogous criteria-exec surface (F-P1-3) as a real latent surface worth gating, and the 015 packet explicitly claimed to fix this exact traversal class — leaving the first writer unguarded is an incomplete remediation, not a non-issue.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if a documented invariant guarantees fixture ids are schema-validated against SAFE_FIXTURE_ID upstream (e.g. at profile load / fixture authoring) before materialization, OR if the materializer is provably never run on untrusted fixtures in any shipped path.
